overview: This step creates the target segment container for each text unit in the pipeline, optionally copying source content and/or properties into the new target. It is typically placed early in a pipeline before any translation or leveraging steps, ensuring that a target entry exists for downstream processing. Takes filter events and sends filter events.

copyContent.help:
Copies the source text into the newly created target segment. This is useful as a starting point before translation or leveraging steps modify the target content.

When used together with **Copy the source properties to the target**, both content and metadata are duplicated.

copyProperties.help: Copies source-level properties (metadata key-value pairs attached to the text unit) into the target. Properties may include annotations, notes, or custom attributes set by earlier pipeline steps or the filter.

overwriteExisting.help: Forces the source content and/or properties to be copied into the target **even if a target already exists**. Without this option, text units that already have a target entry (e.g., from a bilingual input format) are left unchanged.
notes: **Warning:** Enabling this will discard any existing target content, including previously applied translations. Use with care in pipelines that process bilingual files.

dependsOn.condition: only meaningful when at least one of copyContent or copyProperties is true

createOnNonTranslatable.help: Controls whether a target entry is created for text units marked as non-translatable (`translate="no"`). Disabling this skips target creation for non-translatable segments, which can reduce noise in downstream steps that only care about translatable content.

processingNotes: If neither **copyContent** nor **copyProperties** is enabled, the step still creates an empty target container — downstream steps can then populate it.
This step is commonly placed immediately after the Raw Document to Filter Events step and before any leveraging or translation steps.
